task: Write a Python function collapse_delims(s) that splits the string on commas, treats any run of consecutive commas as one split, drops empty pieces at the start and end, and returns the pieces rejoined with a single comma between each. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: Code meets all the specified requirements, including handling a notable edge case where multiple consecutive commas are treated as one.
trace: I used Python's string replace method to eliminate consecutive commas before splitting the string. Then, I filtered out any empty pieces at the start and end by only joining non-empty pieces. To handle the edge case where there may be run of leading or trailing empty strings, I removed them from the list before joining the remaining pieces with a single comma separator.
